task_completion.description: Agent accessed the Django admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Django administration interface.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the 'Add group' page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add group' page in the Django admin panel is visible, showing fields for 'Name' and 'Permissions'.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct group name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Regulatory Auditors' is entered into the 'Name' input field on the Add group page.

task_completion.description: Agent selected the six required view-only permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Chosen permissions' box (on the right side of the permissions widget) contains the six required permissions: 'registry | aircraft | Can view aircraft', 'registry | contact | Can view contact', 'registry | operator | Can view operator', 'registry | person | Can view person', 'gcs_operations | flight operation | Can view flight operation', and 'gcs_operations | flight plan | Can view flight plan'.

task_completion.description: Agent did not select any forbidden permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Chosen permissions' box does not contain any permissions with 'Can add', 'Can change', or 'Can delete'.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the new group successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'The group "Regulatory Auditors" was added successfully.') is visible, or the group 'Regulatory Auditors' is visible in the list of groups.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser being used to navigate the Django admin panel, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the correct local server.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's address bar shows 'localhost:8000/admin' or '127.0.0.1:8000/admin'.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing sqlite3 commands or Python scripts modifying the database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a Django group named 'Regulatory Auditors' via the Django admin panel. The required permissions in the 'Chosen permissions' box should be exactly: 'registry | aircraft | Can view aircraft', 'registry | contact | Can view contact', 'registry | operator | Can view operator', 'registry | person | Can view person', 'gcs_operations | flight operation | Can view flight operation', and 'gcs_operations | flight plan | Can view flight plan'. No permissions with 'Can add', 'Can change', or 'Can delete' should be in the chosen list.
